questions.question: The graph of y = sin(x) has a period of 2π. What is the period of y = sin(2x)?
questions.explanation: When the argument is multiplied by a constant k, the period becomes (original period)/k. Here, period = 2π/2 = π.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 30°, angle B = 60°, and side a = 5. Using the Law of Sines, find side b.
questions.explanation: By Law of Sines: a/sin(A) = b/sin(B), so 5/sin(30°) = b/sin(60°). Thus 5/(1/2) = b/(√3/2), giving b = 5√3.

questions.question: What is the amplitude of the function y = 3cos(x)?
questions.explanation: Amplitude is the absolute value of the coefficient in front of the trig function. Here |3| = 3.

questions.question: In triangle DEF, sides d = 7, e = 8, and angle F = 90°. Find side f using the Law of Cosines.
questions.explanation: Law of Cosines: f² = d² + e² − 2de·cos(F) = 49 + 64 − 2(7)(8)cos(90°) = 113 + 0 = 113. So f = √113.

questions.question: The graph of y = tan(x) has a vertical asymptote at x = π/2. At what x-value does the next vertical asymptote occur?
questions.explanation: The tangent function has vertical asymptotes at odd multiples of π/2. The next asymptote after π/2 is at 3π/2.

questions.question: A triangle has sides a = 6, b = 8, and c = 10. Is this a right triangle? Explain briefly.
questions.explanation: Check if a² + b² = c²: 36 + 64 = 100 ✓. Since the condition holds, this is a right triangle with the right angle opposite the side of length 10.
